meaning: n.平民,庶民adj.平民的,卑俗的
examples: It was a boy, bareheaded, ill shod, and clothed in coarse plebeian garments that were falling to rags.
Some mass media tend to please people of plebeian tastes.
When did fly-fishing come to be regarded as a patrician activity and coarse-fishing as a plebeian one?
It was only half a century later, as the consequences of pushing the earth out into plebeian orbit dawned on the priests, that it became too hot to handle, or even touch.
